evidence: We use AI and other emerging technologies in various facets of our operations and our business.
failure to effectively and timely utilize, emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence
We use AI and other emerging technologies in various facets of our operations and our business.
unable to timely utilize AI for technological innovation and business operation efficiency in a manner that is faster and more effective than our competitors
compliance with new or changing laws, regulations or industry standards relating to AI may impose significant costs on us and limit our ability to effectively develop, deploy or use AI technologies
if we are unable to effectively manage the use of AI technologies by our employees and service providers, our confidential information, intellectual property and reputation could be put at risk
cybersecurity threats and the techniques used in cybersecurity attacks change, develop, and evolve rapidly, including from emerging technologies, such as advanced forms of AI and quantum computing

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI spend, headcount, or named vendor/infrastructure partnerships disclosed in excerpts; generic operational usage only.
ai_narrative_concreteness: No named AI initiatives, specific use cases, or deployment details provided; language is aspirational and risk-framed rather than descriptive of concrete programs.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and discusses AI risks with company-specific mechanisms (regulatory cost, IP/confidentiality exposure, competitive disadvantage, cybersecurity via AI-enhanced attacks), warranting score 4; no quantified risk figures or named governance bodies found to justify score 5.
